title: Go 1.26 and AWS SDK for Go v2 Client Contract · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent team receives different tenants, file sizes, media types, regions, compliance duties, traffic, failures, and cost limits and transfers go 1.26 and aws sdk for go v2 client contract into a new pinned Go and AWS SDK v2 client contract. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of pinned Go and AWS SDK v2 client contract with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evi…
objectives: Pin Go 1.26.5, aws-sdk-go-v2 1.42.1, S3 1.105.0, CloudFront sign 1.11.7, and tested operating targets as one compatibility contract.
Load immutable shared configuration once, construct scoped clients, pass context, and assign transport and credential lifetimes.
Use SDK pointers, enums, document types, output metadata, paginators, and nil handling without losing API semantics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Runtime Sdk Version
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go file-delivery team handles case 6173 during a private media release. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce pinned Go and AWS SDK v2 versions, client lifetime, runtime validation, and typed errors; retain stable tenant and file identity without exposing it in metric labels; then change region, range, or signed header and explain the exact failure boundary. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Pin Go 1.26.5, aws-sdk-go-v2 1.42.1, S3 1.105.0, CloudFront sign 1.11.7, and tested operating targets as one compatibility contract.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Runtime Sdk Version.

For S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Runtime Sdk Version, keep Runtime Sdk Version connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Sdk Error Interface
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: storage-go-sdk-error-interface-1fx1ix" that uses case 6224 to enforce pinned Go and AWS SDK v2 versions, client lifetime, runtime validation, and typed errors. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import AWS SDK packages, open sockets, contact S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, networks, databases, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5 compile, vet, fuzz, race, leak, controlled AWS, load, fault, security, cost, restore, and production gates.
